questions.question_text: An out-of-state developer divides land into 30 lots and wants to sell them to Nebraska residents. What must the developer obtain from the NREC?
distractors: A broker's license only
A zoning variance from the county
A plat approval from the county
questions.explanation: Under Nebraska's subdivided land registration laws, land outside Nebraska divided into 25 or more lots for sale to Nebraska residents requires a subdivision certificate from the NREC before sales may begin.

questions.question_text: A developer registers 200 out-of-state lots with the NREC. What is the total registration fee?
questions.explanation: The NREC registration fee is $100 plus $25 per 100 lots or fraction thereof. For 200 lots: $100 base + $25 for the first 100 + $25 for the second 100 = $150.

questions.question_text: A developer divides out-of-state land into 20 lots for sale to Nebraska residents. Is NREC subdivision registration required?
questions.correct_answer: No, the threshold is 25 or more lots
distractors: Yes, any subdivision requires it
Yes, the threshold is 10 lots
Only if the lots exceed one acre each
questions.explanation: Nebraska's subdivided land registration applies to out-of-state land divided into 25 or more lots for sale to Nebraska residents. A 20-lot development falls below this threshold.

questions.question_text: Who is authorized to sell registered subdivided land in Nebraska on behalf of an out-of-state developer?
distractors: Any licensed agent from any state
The developer without a license
A Nebraska-licensed attorney
questions.explanation: Nebraska law requires all sales of registered subdivided land to be conducted through a Nebraska-licensed real estate broker, ensuring compliance with state consumer protections.

questions.question_text: A developer registers out-of-state lots for sale to Nebraska residents. Which type of land must the lots be?
questions.correct_answer: Platted land with plats filed at the county
distractors: Any land with a legal description
Land surveyed within the last 5 years
Land zoned for residential use only
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires out-of-state subdivided land sold to Nebraska residents to be platted with plats filed at the local county authority. Unplatted land cannot be registered for sale to Nebraska buyers.

questions.question_text: A company plans to sell time-share interests in a Nebraska resort. Before offering units, what must the company do?
questions.correct_answer: Register the time-shares with the NREC
distractors: Obtain a special resort license
File with the Secretary of State only
Get approval from the local zoning board
questions.explanation: Time-share interests must be registered with the NREC before they can be offered for sale in Nebraska, ensuring the offering meets state regulatory standards.

questions.question_text: A real estate licensee sells memberships in a campground. Must this licensee also register as a campground salesperson?
distractors: Yes, a separate registration is required
Only if selling more than 10 memberships
Only if the campground is out of state
questions.explanation: Under the Nebraska Membership Campground Act, real estate licensees are exempt from the separate campground salesperson registration requirement.

questions.question_text: A retirement community developer wants to market units in Nebraska. What must the developer do before offering units?
questions.correct_answer: Register the community with the NREC
distractors: Obtain approval from the county only
File a prospectus with the SEC
Get a special developer license from HUD
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires retirement subdivisions and communities to register with the NREC before offering units for sale. This registration ensures the development meets state consumer protection standards.

questions.question_text: A membership campground operator wants to market in Nebraska. What registration is required?
distractors: Obtain a broker's license from NREC
File a subdivision certificate only
No registration is required
questions.explanation: Membership campgrounds must register under the Nebraska Membership Campground Act before they can be offered or sold in Nebraska.

questions.question_text: A developer offers time-shares in another state to Nebraska buyers without registering with the NREC. What is the status of these offerings?
distractors: They are legal if federally registered
They are permitted for out-of-state units
They are legal if sold by NE brokers
questions.explanation: Time-shares must be registered with the NREC before being offered for sale in Nebraska, regardless of the property's physical location. Failing to register violates state law.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state developer contracts to sell lots to Nebraska residents. In what form must the sales contracts be?
distractors: Notarized but not recordable
Any written format is acceptable
NREC-approved template only
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires contracts for out-of-state land sales to Nebraska residents to be in recordable form, providing buyers with the ability to record their interest in the property.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state developer promises to build roads and utilities in a subdivision marketed to Nebraska buyers. How must the developer guarantee completion?
distractors: A letter of intent
A personal guarantee
An escrow deposit of 5%
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires out-of-state developers to demonstrate their ability to perform on promised improvements via performance bonds, protecting Nebraska buyers from unfulfilled promises.

questions.question_text: An Arizona developer hires a salesperson licensed only in Arizona to sell lots to Nebraska residents. Is this arrangement lawful?
questions.correct_answer: No, sales must go through a NE broker
distractors: Yes, if the lots are in Arizona
Yes, with NREC written approval
Only if co-brokering with a NE agent
questions.explanation: All sales of out-of-state land to Nebraska residents must be conducted through a Nebraska-licensed broker and their employees. An Arizona-only licensee cannot sell directly to Nebraska buyers.

questions.question_text: A Colorado developer sells lots to Nebraska buyers but never had the land platted. Which Nebraska requirement has the developer violated?
questions.correct_answer: Land must be platted with plats on file
distractors: The performance bond requirement
The NREC advertising disclosure rule
The minimum lot-size standard
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires out-of-state land sold to Nebraska residents to be platted, with the plats filed at the county level. Selling unplatted lots violates this requirement.

questions.question_text: A developer sells out-of-state lots to Nebraska residents using an oral contract. A buyer later disputes the sale. What is the most likely outcome?
questions.correct_answer: The contract fails the recordable form rule
distractors: The contract is enforceable if witnessed
The oral agreement is valid under NE law
The buyer must prove fraud to void it
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires contracts for out-of-state land sales to be in recordable form. An oral contract does not meet this standard and would likely be unenforceable.
